thread.title: “I’m from here, y’all.” Trans groups organize Huntsville rally
thread.title_full: “I’m from here, y’all.” Trans groups organize Huntsville rally

author: @waff48
title: “I’m from here, y’all.” Trans groups organize Huntsville rally
text:
“I’m from here, y’all.” Trans groups organize Huntsville rally
HUNTSVILLE, Ala. ([WAFF](http://www.waff.com)) - Several transgender rights groups organized a rally at Huntsville’s Stoner Park Saturday, saying they’re a part of this community and not going anywhere.
“It’s critically important for people to know that we are here. Trans Alabamians do exist“ organizer Allison Montgomery told WAFF 48. “Some people think there aren’t any trans people in Alabama or that we are foreign somehow. But, I’m from here, y’all.”
The event included education booths, food, music and free clothes for transitioning members of the community. It’s an event that organizers say is more important now than ever.
“As trans people, we can have difficulty finding community, and so gathering in person is really helpful finding other people with shared experiences.“ Montgomery said. Fellow organizer Izze Saint-Sykes added “We want to make sure everybody knows there are people who care about them, and their experiences and concerns where other folks have fallen short maybe, lately.”
A small group of protestors made themselves known nearby, but Saint-Sykes dismissed them when asked. “That’s always going to happen. There’s always going to be somebody who doesn’t like what you do, or doesn’t like how you live. They’re just nosy. The best thing you can do is do your best to live as you are and ignore what they think. At the end of the day, they don’t matter.”
The event was sponsored by four different groups: “Rainbow Trouble”, “The Weird Kids”, “Trans North Alabama” and “The Alabama Transgender Rights Action Coalition".
